_meta.version: v2 (refined from v1's 10 papers to 26 papers)

writing_patterns.description: To argue for a design, He tests it against an extreme or degenerate case: what happens if identity mapping is optimal, if momentum is zero, if masking ratio is 100%, if a single noise level is used. These boundary cases reveal the mechanism and expose what prior work left unexplained.

evidence_quotes.quote: When m 𝑚 m is too small ( e.g . , 0.9), the accuracy drops considerably; at the extreme of no momentum ( m 𝑚 m is 0), the training loss oscillates and fails to converge.
if identity mappings are optimal, the solvers may simply drive the weights of the multiple nonlinear layers toward zero to approach identity mappings.

Using this single-level noise achieves decent accuracy of 61.5%, a 3% degradation vs. the multi-level noise counterpart (64.5%). Using multiple levels of noise is analogous to a form of data augmentation in DAE: it is beneficial, but not an enabling factor.

writing_patterns.description: The Method section opens by identifying a widely-held assumption or dominant practice as the thing being challenged, then constructs a solution directly from first principles as a counter-argument. The reader is positioned as witnessing a conceptual correction rather than an incremental extension.

evidence_quotes.quote: It is widely believed that noise conditioning is indispensable for denoising diffusion models to work successfully. This work challenges this belief.
Today's denoising diffusion models do not "denoise" in the classical sense, i.e., they do not directly predict clean images.

Standard NFs consist of a forward process and a reverse process... Typical NF forward transformations are constrained by explicit invertibility... In this work, we introduce Bidirectional Normalizing Flow (BiFlow), a framework that removes the need for an exact analytic inverse.

writing_patterns.description: He opens the core method argument not with a claim about performance but with a thought experiment or boundary-case construction that shows the proposed solution must exist or must be at least as good as a known baseline. This grounds the method in deductive reasoning before any empirical claim is made.

evidence_quotes.quote: There exists a solution by construction to the deeper model: the added layers are identity mapping, and the other layers are copied from the learned shallower model. The existence of this constructed solution indicates that a deeper model should produce no higher training error than its shallower counterpart.

We hypothesize that it is easier to optimize the residual mapping than to optimize the original, unreferenced mapping. To the extreme, if an identity mapping were optimal, it would be easier to push the residual to zero than to fit an identity mapping by a stack of nonlinear layers.

writing_patterns.description: A toy or controlled experiment is placed within the Method or early Results section—before full-scale experiments—to demonstrate the core claim in a clean, interpretable setting. This establishes intuition and reduces the chance that positive results are attributed to confounding factors.

evidence_quotes.quote: We verify this assumption in a toy experiment in this section. We consider the toy case of d-dimensional underlying data 'buried' in an observed D-dimensional space.
To corroborate these theoretical findings, we empirically run a simulation on a real dataset and plot p(t|z). The empirical distribution of p(t|z) is well concentrated.

We consider a pseudo-dataset classification task. In this scenario, we manually create multiple pseudo-datasets, all of which are sampled without replacement from the same source dataset. We expect this process to give us multiple pseudo-datasets that are truly unbiased.

writing_patterns.description: Complex systems are architecturally decoupled into separate, independently motivated components—typically a forward model and a reverse or inference model—so that each can be designed and justified on its own terms. This pattern appears across papers ranging from normalizing flows to generative models to reasoning systems.

evidence_quotes.quote: In our framework, the designs of the forward and reverse processes are decoupled: the forward process can be any NF model that is computable, tractable, and easy to learn, while the reverse process learns a separate model to approximate its inverse.
We adopt a two-stage training paradigm to learn the parameters of the neural network.

Training is performed in two stages: first, similar to classical NF, we train the forward model using maximum likelihood estimation; then, keeping the forward model fixed, we train the reverse model to approximate its inverse mapping.

writing_patterns.description: He frequently separates two things that prior work conflated—dictionary size from batch size, mask prediction from class prediction, encoder from decoder, AR from VQ—and argues each independently. The decoupling is always motivated by showing that the coupling creates an unnecessary constraint.

evidence_quotes.quote: The queue decouples the dictionary size from the mini-batch size, allowing it to be large.

Our definition of L m ​ a ​ s ​ k subscript 𝐿 𝑚 𝑎 𝑠 𝑘 L_{mask} allows the network to generate masks for every class without competition among classes; we rely on the dedicated classification branch to predict the class label used to select the output mask. This decouples mask and class prediction.

The MAE decoder is only used during pre-training to perform the image reconstruction task (only the encoder is used to produce image representations for recognition). Therefore, the decoder architecture can be flexibly designed in a manner that is independent of the encoder design.

writing_patterns.description: He explicitly reports negative or surprising results—cases where his method fails, where removing a component unexpectedly hurts, or where the expected benefit does not materialize—and does not bury them. These findings are often given their own subsection or a dedicated sentence with precise numbers.

evidence_quotes.quote: But there are still open problems on such aggressively deep models. The testing result of this 1202-layer network is worse than that of our 110-layer network, although both have similar training error. We argue that this is because of overfitting.

Interestingly, its detection accuracy is higher than that of using the MLP alone, Table 1 (b) vs . (a), despite much lower linear classification accuracy (63.4% vs . 66.2%). This indicates that linear classification accuracy is not monotonically related to transfer performance in detection.
On the negative side, our algorithm still makes mistakes in cases that are not difficult for humans, especially for those requiring context understanding or high-level knowledge

writing_patterns.description: Surprising or negative results are explicitly flagged with phrases like 'surprisingly', 'unexpectedly', or 'contrary to common belief', and are reported with the same completeness as positive results. The paper treats the unexpected finding as the scientific contribution rather than a liability to be minimized.

evidence_quotes.quote: Contrary to common belief, noise conditioning is not an enabling factor for most denoising-based models to function properly. Most variants can work gracefully, exhibiting small but decent degradation.
To our (and many of our initial readers') surprise, modern neural networks can achieve excellent accuracy on such a dataset classification task.
Even more surprisingly, we find that, conversely, introducing a bottleneck that reduces dimensionality in the network can be beneficial.

writing_patterns.description: Hyperparameters are not tuned extensively nor presented as critical; instead, experiments are designed to show robustness across a range of values, and default values are fixed and reported. This communicates that the method is not over-fitted to any particular setting.

evidence_quotes.quote: We compare a wide range of temperature values τ and find that the results are surprisingly robust: this is in contrary to contrastive self-supervised learning, where performance degrades significantly when deviating from the optimal temperature.

We conduct a sweep over the guidance scale ω and temperature τ to determine the optimal combination for each model variant. This sweep is performed for models with 160M and 360M parameters, and we find that the optimal parameters remain consistent across these two scales.
GN performs reasonably well for all values of G we studied. In the extreme case of G=1, GN is equivalent to LN, and its error rate is higher than all cases of G>1 studied.

writing_patterns.description: New hyperparameters are introduced one at a time, immediately followed by their default value and the ablation that justifies the choice. He does not list all hyperparameters together in an implementation section; instead, each hyperparameter appears at the point in the narrative where its role is first explained.

evidence_quotes.quote: Here m ∈ [ 0 , 1 ) 𝑚 0 1 m\in[0,1) is a momentum coefficient. Only the parameters θ q subscript 𝜃 q \theta_{\textrm{q}} are updated by back-propagation. The momentum update in Eqn.( 2 ) makes θ k subscript 𝜃 k \theta_{\textrm{k}} evolve more smoothly than θ q subscript 𝜃 q \theta_{\textrm{q}} .
where τ 𝜏 \tau is a temperature hyper-parameter per [ 61 ] .

We set hyper-parameters following existing Fast/Faster R-CNN work [12, 36, 27]. Although these decisions were made for object detection in original papers [12, 36, 27], we found our instance segmentation system is robust to them.

writing_patterns.description: He derives hyperparameter choices (masking ratio, latent dimension) from an explicit, falsifiable hypothesis about the data modality (information redundancy), then validates the hypothesis by comparing numbers across domains (language 15%, images 75%, video 90%). The hypothesis is stated as a hypothesis, not a claim.

evidence_quotes.quote: It is hypothesized in [ 31 ] that the masking ratio ( i.e . , percentage of removed tokens) in masked autoencoding methods is related to the information redundancy of the problems. For example, natural images are more information-redundant than languages and thus the optimal masking ratio is higher ( e.g . , than BERT [ 15 ] ).
The optimal ratios are surprisingly high. The ratio of 75% is good for both linear probing and fine-tuning. This behavior is in contrast with BERT [14], whose typical masking ratio is 15%.

writing_patterns.description: Mathematical derivations in the method section invoke standard calculus tools (product rule, fundamental theorem, chain rule, total derivative) explicitly by name or by demonstrated step. This grounds novel formulations in well-understood operations, making them verifiable and resistant to doubts about soundness.

evidence_quotes.quote: Now we differentiate both sides with respect to t, treating r as independent of t. This leads to... where the manipulation of the left hand side employs the product rule and the right hand side uses the fundamental theorem of calculus.
With d z_t/dt = v(z_t, t)... and dt/dt = 1, we have another relation between u and v... This equation shows that the total derivative is given by the Jacobian-vector product.
Formally, optimizing the loss in Eq. 2 is equivalent to optimizing the following loss, where each term inside the expectation has a unique effective target.

writing_patterns.description: Mathematical derivations appear not as display of rigor but as direct justifications for specific design choices. The derivation is stopped as soon as the desired property (non-vanishing gradient, consistent initialization scale) is established; the results are immediately translated into a practical recipe.

evidence_quotes.quote: This product is the key to the initialization design. A proper initialization method should avoid reducing or magnifying the magnitudes of input signals exponentially. So we expect the above product to take a proper scalar ( e.g . , 1). A sufficient condition is:
This implies that the gradient of a layer does not vanish even when the weights are arbitrarily small.

writing_patterns.description: Before introducing any equation or architecture, a key empirical or theoretical observation is stated and used to justify the proposed design. The observation is framed as sufficient motivation; the method follows as the natural consequence rather than an arbitrary design choice.

evidence_quotes.quote: Under this manifold assumption, while clean data can be modeled as lying on a low-dimensional manifold, a noised quantity is inherently distributed across the full high-dimensional space. Predicting clean data is fundamentally different from predicting noise or a noised quantity.

The channels of visual representations are not entirely independent. Classical features of SIFT, HOG, and GIST are group-wise representations by design, where each group of channels is constructed by some kind of histogram.

Solely originated from this definition, we derive a well-defined, intrinsic relation between the average and instantaneous velocities, which naturally serves as a principled basis for guiding network training.

writing_patterns.description: He positions his design against the nearest prior work inside the Method section itself—not just in Related Work—with a side-by-side comparison that identifies the single key difference. This immediately answers 'why not just use X?' without requiring readers to cross-reference another section.

evidence_quotes.quote: Unlike RoIAlign, RoIWarp overlooked the alignment issue and was implemented in [10] as quantizing RoI just like RoIPool. So even though RoIWarp also adopts bilinear resampling motivated by [22], it performs on par with RoIPool as shown by experiments

The memory bank [ 61 ] mechanism can support a larger dictionary size. But it is 2.6% worse than MoCo. This is inline with our hypothesis: the keys in the memory bank are from very different encoders all over the past epoch and they are not consistent.

writing_patterns.description: Prior methods are presented as addressing the right problem but with a limitation or blind spot rather than being fundamentally mistaken. This rhetorical move allows the paper to build on prior work while carving out a distinct contribution, and avoids overclaiming novelty.

evidence_quotes.quote: As a pioneering effort, REPA has revealed the importance of representation learning in the context of generative modeling; however, its instantiation depends on additional pre-training, extra model parameters, and access to external data.

Consistency Models are focused on paths anchored at the data side... On the other hand, the Shortcut and IMM models are conditioned on two time variables... In contrast, our method is solely driven by the definition of average velocity.
Despite its great success, BN exhibits drawbacks that are also caused by its distinct behavior of normalizing along the batch dimension.

writing_patterns.description: Each major design choice is preceded by an explicit statement of the problem or phenomenon that motivated it, often naming the problem before naming the fix. This creates a clear causal chain: observation → diagnosis → design, and prevents readers from encountering solutions without knowing what they solve.

evidence_quotes.quote: In principle Mask R-CNN is an intuitive extension of Faster R-CNN, yet constructing the mask branch properly is critical for good results. Most importantly, Faster R-CNN was not designed for pixel-to-pixel alignment between network inputs and outputs.

Our hypothesis is that good features can be learned by a large dictionary that covers a rich set of negative samples, while the encoder for the dictionary keys is kept as consistent as possible despite its evolution.
We ask: what makes masked autoencoding different between vision and language? We attempt to answer this question from the following perspectives

writing_patterns.description: For methods with a training loop, He provides a compact pseudo-code or algorithm box (often just a few lines) as an authoritative implementation reference. The pseudo-code is not ornamental—it resolves ambiguities about stop-gradient placement, queue operations, and symmetrization that prose alone cannot.

evidence_quotes.quote: Algorithm 1 provides the pseudo-code of MoCo for this pretext task. For the current mini-batch, we encode the queries and their corresponding keys, which form the positive sample pairs. The negative samples are from the queue.
The pseudo-code of SimSiam is in Algorithm 1.

writing_patterns.description: Pseudocode or algorithm blocks are used at the close of the method derivation to summarize the training or sampling procedure concisely. This signals that the method is implementable and complete, and allows a reader to verify that the mathematical derivation maps correctly onto a practical procedure.

evidence_quotes.quote: Pseudocode for minimizing the loss function Eq. 9 is presented in Alg. 1. Overall, our method is conceptually simple: it behaves similarly to Flow Matching, with the key difference that the matching target is modified.

Alg. 1 shows the pseudo-code of a training step, and Alg. 2 is that of a sampling step (Euler solver; can be extended to Heun or other solvers). For brevity, class conditioning and CFG are omitted, but both follow standard practice.

Algorithm 2 presents the training pseudo-code, with a specific form of Dispersive Loss as defined in Algorithm 1. We believe that such simplicity greatly facilitates the practical adoption of our methodology.

writing_patterns.description: A known computational primitive (layer, block, model) is abstracted upward and then applied recursively to itself, generating a new level of organization. The method section frames this move as a natural application of modularization principles already present in the field.

evidence_quotes.quote: The key idea behind fractal generative models is to recursively construct more advanced generative models from existing atomic generative modules.

By recursively calling such a divide-and-conquer process, our fractal framework can efficiently handle the joint distribution of k^n variables using n levels of autoregressive models, each operating on a manageable sequence length k.

Because each generator level can produce multiple outputs from a single input, a fractal framework can achieve an exponential increase in generated outputs while only requiring a linear number of recursive levels.

writing_patterns.description: The proposed method is introduced by describing what it removes from existing methods rather than what it adds. 'Without X' or 'no X required' is used to emphasize self-containment and minimalism, making the contribution appear harder to achieve because it accomplishes more with less.

evidence_quotes.quote: Our approach is self-contained and does not rely on any pre-training or auxiliary loss — no latent tokenizer, no adversarial loss, no perceptual loss (thus no pre-trained classifier), and no representation alignment (thus no self-supervised pre-training).

Our method is self-contained and minimalist. In particular, it does not alter the implementation of the original diffusion loss term: it introduces no additional sampling views, no extra data augmentation.

MeanFlow demonstrates strong empirical performance: it achieves an FID of 3.43 with a single function evaluation (1-NFE) on ImageNet 256×256 trained from scratch, significantly outperforming previous state-of-the-art one-step diffusion/flow models.

writing_patterns.description: He builds new methods by removing components from a complex prior system rather than stacking new components on top of a simple baseline. This rhetorical move simultaneously simplifies the design and directly answers 'what is essential?' rather than 'what helps?'.

evidence_quotes.quote: In a nutshell, our method can be thought of as "BYOL without the momentum encoder". Unlike BYOL but like SimCLR and SwAV, our method directly shares the weights between the two branches, so it can also be thought of as "SimCLR without negative pairs"

Our philosophy is to deconstruct a DDM, gradually transforming it into a classical Denoising Autoencoder (DAE). This deconstructive procedure allows us to explore how various components of modern DDMs influence self-supervised representation learning.

writing_patterns.description: After introducing a general framework or operation, multiple instantiations or variants are enumerated (often 3-4), with the explicit expectation that experiments will show robustness across them. This pattern both demonstrates generality and pre-empts the objection that the method only works for one specific design choice.

evidence_quotes.quote: Next we describe several versions of f and g. Interestingly, we will show by experiments (Table 2a) that our non-local models are not sensitive to these choices, indicating that the generic non-local behavior is the main reason for the observed improvements.

Conceptually, Dispersive Loss can be derived from any existing contrastive loss by appropriately removing the positive terms. In this regard, the term 'Dispersive Loss' does not refer to a specific implementation, but rather to a general class of objectives that encourage dispersion.
These four variants each employing different combinations of these two factors. We scale their parameters from 150M to 3B and evaluate their performance using three metrics.

writing_patterns.description: He codifies architecture decisions as numbered, named rules or invariants that the rest of the design must satisfy, making implicit engineering choices legible and reproducible. These rules are often simple (e.g., double channels when halving resolution) but their explicit statement distinguishes principled from ad-hoc design.

evidence_quotes.quote: The convolutional layers mostly have 3×3 filters and follow two simple design rules: (i) for the same output feature map size, the layers have the same number of filters; and (ii) if the feature map size is halved, the number of filters is doubled so as to preserve the time complexity per layer.

For clarity, we differentiate between: (i) the convolutional backbone architecture used for feature extraction over an entire image, and (ii) the network head for bounding-box recognition (classification and regression) and mask prediction that is applied separately to each RoI.

anti_patterns_kaiming_would_flag: Claim generality but ablate only one dataset or one model size.
Claim method novelty while omitting a within-Method comparison to the most similar prior work.
Claim training from scratch is hard without providing normalization and schedule controls.
Conflate loss-space reweighting with prediction-target change as if they are equivalent.
Conflate two independently controllable axes (e.g., batch size and number of negatives) and treat them as one.
Define a general framework but instantiate it with only one variant in all experiments.
Describe the method only as additions to a baseline without exploring what can be removed.
Explain a design choice solely by appeal to prior art rather than by first-principles reasoning.
Frame the method section as a list of components without connecting them through a unifying hypothesis.
Hide negative or unexpected results in appendices or omit them entirely.
Introduce a construction-argument baseline (identity mapping, random initialization) without testing it empirically.
Introduce a new component without stating which specific failure it addresses.
Introduce multiple new hyperparameters together without per-hyperparameter ablations.
Motivate a design choice only after presenting it, not before.
Position the contribution as novel without acknowledging the closest prior work explicitly.
Present a hyperparameter sweep as a finding rather than a robustness check.
Present a new component without first naming the problem it solves.
Present hyperparameter sensitivity results only for the method's favorable range, hiding fragility.
Present the method as a list of engineering tricks rather than a principled solution.
Provide math derivations that are longer than needed to justify the resulting recipe.
Rely on pre-trained external models without reporting what happens without them.
Report only best results and omit failure cases, surprising negative findings, or degraded configurations.
State a design rule as self-evident engineering rather than deriving or empirically validating it.
State an error bound without showing it correlates with empirical behavior.
Use 'simple' or 'straightforward' without showing the simplicity concretely.
Use pseudo-code as decoration rather than as an unambiguous specification of critical operations like stop-gradient.
Write pseudocode that omits the stop-gradient or other critical implementation detail.
review_checklist: Are all hyperparameter sensitivity experiments shown across the full range, not only near the optimum?
Are architecture design rules stated explicitly as numbered invariants rather than implicit conventions?
Are decoupling moves clearly labeled, with the removed constraint explicitly named?
Are mathematical derivations terminated as soon as the desired design recipe is established?
Are nearest prior methods compared within the Method section, not only in Related Work?
Check that claimed generality is tested on at least two architectures or two datasets.
Check that every design choice is motivated before it is introduced, not after.
Check that the conclusion of the method section links back to the opening challenge.
Check that the method can be described in subtraction terms (what it removes from prior art).
Check that when multiple instantiations are proposed, ablations cover all of them.
Confirm at least one toy or controlled experiment isolates the core claim cleanly.
Confirm hyperparameter robustness is demonstrated over a range, not tuned to one value.
Confirm that any self-supervised or representation-learning auxiliary requires no external data, or the dependency is explicitly justified and ablated.
Does pseudo-code or an algorithm box exist for any method with a non-trivial training loop?
Does the method description remain self-contained enough that a reader unfamiliar with the cited baselines can follow the reasoning?
Does the paper use subtraction (removing components from a complex prior) at least once to identify essentials?
Does the section open with a problem statement, observation, or thought experiment rather than a summary of the approach?
Ensure all math derivations cite the specific calculus rule or theorem being applied.
Ensure unexpected or negative results are reported with the same completeness as positive ones.
Is each major design choice preceded by a one-sentence diagnosis of the problem it addresses?
Is every new hyperparameter introduced with its default value and a supporting ablation?
Is the information-redundancy or scaling hypothesis (when applicable) stated as a testable hypothesis with cross-domain validation?
Is there at least one constructed-solution or boundary-case argument grounding the core design claim?
Is there at least one honest report of a negative or surprising finding with specific numbers?
Verify pseudocode or an algorithm block faithfully reflects the mathematical derivation.
Verify that decoupled components are each independently motivated before being combined.
Verify that prior work is positioned as incomplete or limited, not fundamentally wrong.
Verify the method section opens by identifying the specific assumption being challenged.
